forecasts.reasoning: After comprehensive analysis, the probability that Ramzan Kadyrov will cease to be head of the Chechen Republic before June 1, 2025 is 8%. Key factors: (1) Historical pattern of public statements about stepping down followed by immediate retractions (2016, 2017, 2022); (2) May 6-7 pattern exactly repeats this - Kadyrov asked to be "relieved of duties" then clarified/walked it back same day; (3) No official Kremlin/Putin acceptance by May 8; (4) Extremely tight 3-week timeline for formal transition; (5) Putin's strategic interest in stability; (6) Health rumors persist but unconfirmed and Kadyrov continues public appearances; (7) Succession planning discussions about son Adam appear speculative; (8) Most recent source (Hudson Institute, May 9) still describes him as "the leader of Chechnya" with no indication of actual departure. The weight of evidence suggests this is political signaling rather than genuine intent for imminent transition.

The question resolves YES if NFL approves a rule restricting the Tush Push before June 1, 2025. Current evidence strongly suggests this will happen: (1) CONFIRMED TIMING: May 20-21 meeting is 10 days before the June 1 deadline (search_1_result_0, search_1_result_1), (2) EXPANDED SCOPE: The proposal shifted from targeting only Tush Push to banning ALL pushing/pulling ball carriers, which may broaden support (search_1_result_2, search_0_summary), (3) STRENGTHENING MOMENTUM: Early April showed 16-16 split (search_3_result_1, search_2_result_4), but by May 4 Washington Post reported 'strong support expected' for expanded version (search_1_result_2), and May 12 Northeast Times stated 24 teams expected to vote yes - exactly the threshold (lookup_0, search_2_result_3), (4) OFFICIAL SUPPORT: Commissioner Goodell and Competition Committee support the change (search_0_summary, search_2_summary), (5) NO INJURY DATA: While no injury data supports ban (search_2_summary), the 'image of the game' argument appears to be winning. The probability balances the strong late momentum (68%) against some uncertainty about whether the expectation will materialize (32% chance it falls short of 24 votes or is delayed). The May 20-21 date provides adequate buffer from June 1 deadline.
